title: Overlap address bytes with slot seed at specific memory offsets for collision-free slot derivation
trigger_patterns: packed address and seed in scratch space
description: A 20-byte address is written at 0x00 and a 4-byte slot seed is written at 0x0c, overlapping the last 8 bytes of the zero-padded address. keccak256(0x0c, 0x20) then hashes the 12 bytes of the address tail plus the 4-byte seed plus 16 bytes of the next word — producing a unique slot per address without needing a full abi.encode. This is distinct from basic scratch space usage: the key insight is the overlapping write positions that pack two values into fewer bytes.
pattern_before:
// Standard mapping slot
bytes32 slot = keccak256(abi.encode(owner, uint256(SLOT_NUMBER)));
pattern_after:
// Overlapping seed packing — 20-byte address + 4-byte seed
mstore(0x0c, _BALANCE_SLOT_SEED) // 4 bytes at 0x0c
mstore(0x00, owner)              // 20 bytes ending at 0x14, overlaps seed
let slot := keccak256(0x0c, 0x20) // hashes from 0x0c to 0x2c
when_to_apply: Any single-key mapping where the key is an address — balance lookups, nonce lookups, single-key permission checks
when_not_to_apply: Double mappings requiring two keys (use 0x34-byte hash instead), or when scratch space is already occupied by outer assembly
